translated: The costumes are so cute.
They're quite simple... Oh, right, and about the comments...
Someone pointed this out to me, and looking at Shizuku's outfit, I see it too.
When I first saw the card illustration, I just thought, "Oh, she's a fairy!"
I was just looking at it thinking how cute it was, but then...
Someone told me that this might actually be Thumbelina.
I knew the title of Thumbelina, of course, but I'd never actually read the story.
So I looked up what kind of story it was. To summarize, a tiny girl born from a flower is named Thumbelina,
and she gets kidnapped and faces all sorts of dangers.
A lot of things happen to her, she really goes through a hard time.
But at the very end, a swa... swallow?
A swallow saves her, and she becomes free. It's a happy ending.
And...
Wait, aren't these swallows?
The black and white.
Now that I think about it, Minori, Airi, and Haruka all look like swallows.
The yellow on the chest looks like a beak. I think whoever noticed that is amazing.
Thanks for telling me.
It really makes so much sense when you look at it that way.
It changes your perspective. There's even a frog here, and a frog appears in the story too.
In Thumbelina.
It really clicked for me. It's so interesting.
The story of Thumbelina is about how if you don't give up,
your dreams will come true and you'll be free. I felt like that's exactly what this story is about.
With the swallows' help, Shizuku is able to spread her wings.
It's so MMJ, honestly.
So that's how it is... Thanks for letting me know.
Anyway, combined with the story of "The Thread That Leads to You,"
the illustrations are just wonderful.
